Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–252, § 1100 Stat. 30Feb. 27, 1986“This Act [enacting this chapter and amending , Food and Drugs] may be cited as the ‘Comprehensive Smokeless Tobacco Health Education Act of 1986’.”section 342 of Title 21

children.label: A
children.text: develop educational programs and materials and public service announcements respecting the dangers to human health from the use of smokeless tobacco;

children.label: B
children.text: make such programs, materials, and announcements available to States, local governments, school systems, the media, and such other entities as the Secretary determines appropriate to further the purposes of this chapter;

children.label: C
children.text: conduct and support research on the effect of smokeless tobacco on human health; and

children.label: D
children.text: collect, analyze, and disseminate information and studies on smokeless tobacco and health.

In developing programs, materials, and announcements under paragraph (1) the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of Education, medical and public health entities, consumer groups, representatives of manufacturers of smokeless tobacco products, and other appropriate entities.
to assist in the development of educational programs and materials and public service announcements respecting the dangers to human health from the use of smokeless tobacco,
to assist in the distribution of such programs, materials, and announcements throughout the States, and
to establish 18 as the minimum age for the purchase of smokeless tobacco.
